title: Alacritty + fcitx5: IME candidate window appears on wrong monitor in multi-monitor setup

comments.body: Fixed this for me by installing the x11-ime-patch crate which handles XIM style selection correctly for multi-monitor setups. Just ran `cargo install x11-ime-patch` and rebuilt alacritty, and the candidate window now appears on the correct monitor.

body: ## Symptom In a multi-monitor environment, when using Japanese input (fcitx5) in Alacritty, the candidate window appears on a different monitor from the one Alacritty is on. Specifically, with Alacritty on the center monitor (primary), the candidate window appears near the right edge of the left monitor. It should appear near the cursor position within the center monitor. In kitty, the candidate window position is not accurate either (it goes to the left edge), but at least it stays within the correct monitor. ## Environment ### Hardware - GPU: NVIDIA GeForce RTX 5090 (Driver 580.119.02) - Monitors: 4 displays, all 3840x2160 (4K) ``` Monitor layout: HDMI-0 (3840x2160+3840+0) <- top DP-0 DP-4(primary) DP-2 <- bottom 3 side by side +0+2160 +3840+2160 +7680+2160 ``` ### Software - OS: NixOS 25.11.20260224.e764fc9 (Xantusia) - Kernel: Linux 6.12.74 - Session: X11 - Window manager: xmonad (via home-manager) - Alacritty: 0.17.0-dev (commit e1779cbc, master branch) - winit: 0.30.12 (Alacritty's dependency) - fcitx5: 5.1.16 - fcitx5-mozc-ut: input engine - libX11: 1.8.12 - XMODIFIERS: `@im=fcitx` - HiDPI: scale_factor=1.5 ## Investigation notes I was unable to identify the exact cause, but sharing what I found. ### winit's XIM style selection winit 0.30.12's XIM style selection logic (`src/platform_impl/linux/x11/ime/input_method.rs`) only tries the following two styles: 1. `XIMPreeditCallbacks | XIMStatusNothing` (0x0402) - OnTheSpot 2. `XIMPreeditNothing | XIMStatusNothing` (0x0408) - Root I added debug output and confirmed that fcitx5 supports the following styles: | Value | Style | | ------ | ---------------------------------------- | | 0x0104 | `XIMPreeditPosition \| XIMStatusArea` | | 0x0404 | `XIMPreeditPosition \| XIMStatusNothing` | | 0x0804 | `XIMPreeditPosition \| XIMStatusNone` | | 0x0408 | `XIMPreeditNothing \| XIMStatusNothing` | | 0x0808 | `XIMPreeditNothing \| XIMStatusNone` | Since fcitx5 does not support `XIMPreeditCallbacks`, winit falls back to `XIMPreeditNothing` (0x0408). In `XIMPreeditNothing` mode, `XNSpotLocation` is not a relevant attribute per the XIM spec, so libX11 may not be sending spot location data to the IM server. However, I could not confirm whether this is the direct cause. ### XSetICValues behavior Setting `XNSpotLocation` via `XSetICValues` in `XIMPreeditNothing` mode succeeds (returns NULL), but fcitx5's candidate window was not positioned correctly. ### XTranslateCoordinates verification fcitx5's XIM frontend (`updateCursorLocation` in `src/frontend/xim/xim.cpp`) uses `xcb_translate_coordinates` to convert client-window-relative coordinates to root window coordinates. I verified with a standalone C program that this translation works correctly: ``` XTranslateCoordinates(xwindow=54525957, root, 564, 52) => (4404, 2238) ``` (4404, 2238) is within the center monitor DP-4 (x=3840-7679, y=2160-4319), so fcitx5 should be able to display the candidate window at the correct position if it receives the correct spot coordinates. ### Alacritty window coordinate verification ``` xwininfo -id 0x3400005: Absolute upper-left X: 3840 Y: 2186 Width: 3840 Height: 2134 XTranslateCoordinates(xwindow, root, 0, 0) => (3840, 2186) XGetWindowAttributes: x=3840 y=2186 Manual walk to root: abs=(3840, 2186) ``` All three methods agreed, so there was no issue with obtaining the window position itself. ### libX11 1.8.2 XNSpotLocation fix libX11 1.8.2 fixed an issue where setting `XNSpotLocation` in OnTheSpot mode was blocked (commit 62c3337d, freedesktop bug #1580). However, this fix only enables mode flags for `XIMPreeditCallbacks` (OnTheSpot) and does not affect `XNSpotLocation` handling in `XIMPreeditNothing` mode. The current environment uses libX11 1.8.12 which includes this fix, but since fcitx5 does not support `XIMPreeditCallbacks`, the fix is not directly relevant. ## Attempted fixes and results ### Attempt 1: Adding global coordinate offset I tried modifying winit's `set_ime_cursor_area` to add the `inner_position_physical()` offset to the spot coordinates, converting them to global coordinates. Result: Failed - The candidate window moved to the right monitor instead. fcitx5 internally uses `xcb_translate_coordinates` to convert window-relative coordinates to global coordinates, so pre-converting to global coordinates in winit caused a double translation, shifting the coordinates even further. ### Attempt 2: Adding XIMPreeditPosition style I tried adding `XIMPreeditPosition` to winit's style selection and setting `XNSpotLocation` as a preedit attribute during IC (Input Context) creation. Result: Failed - Japanese input stopped working entirely. The cause is unknown, but it may be due to missing required attributes for `XIMPreeditPosition` IC creation (such as XNFontSet), or incompatibilities with winit's preedit event handling. ## Comparison with kitty kitty does not use the XIM (X Input Method) protocol. Instead, it uses the IBus (Input Bus) protocol via DBus. ```c // kitty's implementation (glfw/ibus_glfw.c) glfw_dbus_call_method_no_reply(ibus->conn, IBUS_SERVICE, ibus->input_ctx_path, IBUS_INPUT_INTERFACE, "SetCursorLocation", DBUS_TYPE_INT32, &x, DBUS_TYPE_INT32, &y, DBUS_TYPE_INT32, &w, DBUS_TYPE_INT32, &h, DBUS_TYPE_INVALID); ``` With the IBus protocol, cursor position is sent directly to the IM server via DBus, so it does not depend on XIM style selection. kitty's positioning is also not accurate (it goes to the left edge), but at least the correct monitor is used. fcitx5 has an IBus compatibility mode, and kitty communicates with fcitx5 via `GLFW_IM_MODULE=ibus`. ## Notes I could not determine whether this issue lies in Alacritty or in winit. I considered reporting to winit, but since Alacritty is where I observed the problem as an application, I am reporting it here. This may be dependent on the specific hardware setup (multi-monitor + HiDPI) and software combination (fcitx5 + xmonad + NVIDIA), so it may be difficult to reproduce.
